title: Remove -dev command files from prod plugin releases
description: The -dev.md command files ship in prod because ethos has no release-plugin.sh/restore-dev-plugin.sh scripts. Per punt-kit/standards/plugins.md §Release flow: at release time, swap plugin.json to prod name and remove -dev command files from the tagged commit. Then restore dev state on main. Fix: create scripts/release-plugin.sh and scripts/restore-dev-plugin.sh, integrate with punt release.
close_reason: Fixed by PR #39 — release scripts strip -dev files from tags

title: No subcommand-level --help flag parsing
MCP tool consolidation: method-dispatch pattern

Also add slash command wrappers for all MCP tools. 13 attribute tools → 3, 4 ext tools → 1.

title: Go Report Card: reach 100% score

After 'claude plugin install ethos@punt-labs', the user cannot tell whether they got v0.2.0 or v0.3.0 without manually inspecting the cache. Add version output after plugin install step.

title: feat: rich identity list output with columnar formatting
description:
Ethos identity list output is a flat comma-separated string. Biff /who shows aligned columns with headers (handle, tty, repo, idle, status, host). Ethos should match this quality.

## Current behavior
`identity list` returns: `bwk (Brian K), * claude (Claude Agento), ...`
Two-channel: panel shows the same flat string, additionalContext has JSON array.

## Target behavior

### MCP response (handleListIdentities)
Add personality and writing_style fields to the list entry struct. Currently only returns handle, name, kind, active.

### Two-channel display (formatIdentityList in format_output.go)
- Panel (updatedMCPToolOutput): `5 identities, 2 active`
- Context (additionalContext): aligned columnar table like biff:

```
HANDLE     NAME              KIND   PERSONALITY         ACTIVE
bwk        Brian K           agent  kernighan           -
claude     Claude Agento     agent  friendly-direct     *
jfreeman   Jim Freeman       human  principal-engineer  *
mdm        Doug M            agent  mcilroy             -
```

### Files to modify
1. `internal/mcp/tools.go` — handleListIdentities: add personality, writing_style to entry struct
2. `internal/hook/format_output.go` — formatIdentityList: columnar table with alignment
3. `internal/hook/format_output_test.go` — update test for new format

### Acceptance criteria
- Panel shows count summary
- Context shows aligned columnar table with headers
- Columns: HANDLE, NAME, KIND, PERSONALITY, ACTIVE
- Active column shows * for active, - for inactive
- Column widths adapt to content (use max width per column)
- `make check` passes

title: refactor: move iam from identity MCP tool to session

title: feat: rich ext get/list output with key-value table
description: Ext get/list shows 'Extensions' in panel and raw JSON in context. Should show count summary in panel (N namespaces or N keys) and columnar key-value table in context (NAMESPACE, KEY, VALUE for get; NAMESPACE column for list). Use FormatTable.

title: Integration design: ethos + beadle, ethos + biff, ethos + quarry
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and three consumer systems. Critical constraint: ethos must NOT know about beadle, biff, or quarry — dependency flows one way. Consumers use ext/ to store their own data on identities.

Three integrations:

1. ethos + beadle: Agents get email addresses and credentials via ext/beadle. Beadle reads identity (name, handle, email field) from ethos sidecar. Beadle stores GPG keys, IMAP config in ext/beadle. Result: agents can send/receive email as themselves.

2. ethos + biff: Agents get team messaging via ext/biff. Biff reads identity for display name and handle. Biff stores preferred TTY, routing preferences in ext/biff. Result: agents can /write, /read, /talk, /plan, /finger as themselves. Session roster already bridges ethos and biff — needs audit for completeness.

3. ethos + quarry: Agents get persistent memory across sessions. Quarry stores per-agent collection or namespace. Past conversations, decisions, work artifacts searchable as the agent's own memory. This is the least proven integration — quarry currently indexes project files, not agent memories. May need a new quarry feature (agent-scoped collections) or may work with existing registration + namespace conventions.

All three integrations must follow the sidecar contract: consumer reads ethos state from filesystem or MCP tools. Consumer stores its own data in ext/<namespace>. Ethos never imports or calls consumer code. Review punt-kit/standards/ for existing integration patterns and confirm or revise.

Deliverable: design doc per integration, implementation beads, ext schema definitions. No code changes to ethos core — only ext data and consumer-side code.
close_reason: Split into 3 separate beads: ethos-g2f (beadle), ethos-wb4 (biff), ethos-c3v (quarry)

title: refactor: hook standards compliance
Agent team teammates have no ethos session
description:
Agent team teammates have no ethos session.

PROVEN ROOT CAUSE: FindClaudePID() fails for teammates because the binary name is a version number, not 'claude'.

Evidence chain:
1. Hooks DO fire for teammates — quarry SessionStart, beads bd prime, and ethos SessionStart all fire. MCP servers and skills all load. (Tested with diagnostic teammates.)
2. The ethos hook receives a valid session_id in stdin payload: {"session_id":"92438591-...","hook_event_name":"SessionStart",...}
3. But FindClaudePID() in internal/process/tree.go calls isClaudeComm() which checks filepath.Base(comm) == "claude".
4. The lead's claude process was launched via symlink ~/.local/bin/claude → .local/share/claude/versions/2.1.86. ps -o comm shows 'claude'. isClaudeComm returns true. Session created.
5. The teammate's claude process was spawned directly by the lead's binary. ps -o comm shows '/Users/jfreeman/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.86'. filepath.Base returns '2.1.86'. isClaudeComm('2.1.86') returns false.
6. FindClaudePID falls back to os.Getppid() — a transient PID that dies. Session PID file becomes orphaned. ethos session roster returns 'No active session.'

FIX: isClaudeComm (or the full path check in readProc) must also match version-named binaries under the claude versions directory. Biff solved the same problem in DES-011a (find_session_key in src/biff/session_key.py).

Specific code: internal/process/tree.go:64-70 — isClaudeComm needs to match paths containing '/claude/versions/' or version strings like '2.1.86' when the parent path is the claude versions directory.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #126. FindClaudePID now recognizes version-named Claude Code binaries on macOS and Linux.

This fires in all projects including Go, reminding users to run Python tools that don't exist. Fix: replace hardcoded Python gates with language-agnostic make check, per makefile.md standard. Dev-specific conventions should not ship in prod plugin code.

title: Integration design: ethos + quarry — agents with persistent memory
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and quarry. Dependency flows one way: quarry reads ethos, ethos never knows about quarry. This is the least proven integration — needs design exploration before implementation.

What it enables: agents have persistent memory across sessions. Past conversations, decisions, work artifacts, and reasoning are searchable and recallable as the agent's own experience. When Claude starts a new session, it can recall 'I worked on persona animation last week' from quarry.

Integration points:
- Quarry reads identity (handle) from ethos to scope memory to the agent
- Quarry stores collection name, search preferences, retention policy in ext/quarry on the identity
- Memory ingestion: session transcripts, bead history, commit messages, PR summaries attributed to the agent
- Memory retrieval: semantic search scoped to the agent's handle, surfaced at session start or on demand

Open questions:
- Per-agent collection vs. agent-scoped namespace within project collections?
- What gets ingested? Full session transcripts are large. Summaries? Key decisions? Bead close messages?
- How does memory survive across repos? Agent memory is personal, not project-scoped.
- Is quarry the right mechanism? It indexes files on disk today. Agent memory may need a different model (event stream vs. document corpus).
- How does the auto-memory system in Claude Code (.claude/projects/*/memory/) relate? Complement or replace?

Ext schema (ext/quarry):
- collection: quarry collection name for this agent's memory
- retention_days: how long to keep memory (0 = forever)
- auto_ingest: what to auto-capture (sessions, beads, commits, none)

Deliverable: design exploration doc, decision on mechanism, ext schema definition, implementation beads if quarry is confirmed as the right approach. No changes to ethos core.

title: PostToolUse hook error on whoami MCP call

title: punt release: call release-plugin.sh for is_plugin projects, not just is_hybrid

Pure plugin projects like ethos (Go + plugin) also have release scripts but they are never called. This caused v0.4.0 to ship with ethos-dev name and -dev command files. Fix: release.py lines 280 and 750 should check is_plugin too.

title: Hire the team: 6 new agent identities + shared team repo
description: Create 6 new agent identities (djb, adt, ghr, edt, ach, adb) with personalities, writing styles, talents, and Claude Code agent definitions. Create punt-labs/team repo as the single source of truth for all identities. Use git submodule at .punt-labs/ethos/ in each project repo to share the team across 25+ repos. Integrate with punt init for automatic setup. Design doc: docs/persona-animation.md (layer 4: expression). Inspired-by figures: Bernstein (security), Turing (formal methods PM), Hopper (dev tools PM), Tufte (UX), Hamilton (finance/ops), Lovelace (infra).
close_reason: Phases 1-5 complete. Phase 6 (punt init) deferred to separate punt-kit PR.

title: Phase 1: Role as first-class ethos concept
description: Add Role as a first-class concept in ethos. Roles are global (reusable across teams). Storage: .punt-labs/ethos/roles/<name>.yaml with global fallback. Schema: name, responsibilities (list), permissions (list). New internal/role/ package with Store (CRUD, YAML persistence). CLI: ethos role create/list/show/delete. MCP: role tool with method dispatch. Per Z spec (docs/teams.tex): roles keyed by name, global across teams. Delegate to bwk.

title: Phase 2: Team as first-class ethos concept
description: Add Team as a first-class concept. Teams bind identities to roles for a set of repositories. Storage: .punt-labs/ethos/teams/<name>.yaml. Schema: name, repositories (list), members (identity+role bindings), collaborations (from_role, to_role, type). New internal/team/ package with Store, Member, Collaboration structs. Validation enforces Z spec invariants: referential integrity (members reference valid identities and roles), non-empty teams, collaboration validity (both roles filled by members), no self-collaboration. CLI: ethos team create/list/show/add-member/remove-member/add-collab/delete. MCP: team tool with method dispatch. Delegate to bwk.

title: Phase 3: Create punt-labs/team shared identity repo
description: Create the punt-labs/team GitHub repo as the single source of truth for all identities, personalities, writing styles, talents, roles, teams, and agent definitions. Move existing 4 identities (jfreeman, claude, bwk, mdm) from ethos .punt-labs/ethos/. Create 6 new agent identities with full personalities, writing styles, and talents: djb (security, Bernstein), adt (PM grounding, Turing), ghr (PM building blocks, Hopper), edt (UX/visual, Tufte), ach (finance/ops, Hamilton), adb (infra/platform, Lovelace). Create 10 role definitions. Create team definitions (engineering, website). Create .claude/agents/*.md for all 8 agent identities. Structure per build-plan-teams.md.

title: Phase 4: Wire team repo as git submodule
description: Replace .punt-labs/ethos/ contents in ethos repo with git submodule pointing to punt-labs/team. LayeredStore already reads from .punt-labs/ethos/ so no Go code changes needed. Verify: ethos show claude, ethos role list, ethos team show engineering all resolve from submodule. Pilot: add same submodule to biff repo and verify identity resolution works cross-repo.

title: Phase 5: SessionStart agent definition installer
description: Extend SessionStart hook to install agent definitions from the submodule into .claude/agents/. After persona injection, scan .punt-labs/ethos/agents/*.md and copy any missing or changed files to .claude/agents/. Same pattern as vox/biff command deployment. Idempotent: only copy if file doesn't exist or content differs. Log deployed agents to stderr. Do not overwrite user-modified definitions (compare content, not mtime). Delegate to bwk.

title: Phase 6: punt init auto-adds team submodule
description: Update punt init to: (1) add the punt-labs/team git submodule at .punt-labs/ethos/ for punt-labs org repos, (2) scaffold .punt-labs/ethos.yaml with sensible defaults (agent: claude, team: engineering). Update punt audit to check that the submodule exists and is up to date, and that ethos.yaml is present. Separate punt-kit PR. Changes in src/punt_kit/init.py and src/punt_kit/audit.py.

title: feat: implement LayeredStore (repo-local → user-global)
description: LayeredStore wraps repo + global Store instances. Load checks repo first, fallback to global. Ext always global. Includes layered attribute resolution and ValidateRefs across both roots.

title: SessionStart hook error: installed plugin is v0.2.0, binary is v0.3.0
description: Root cause: punt release built and tagged v0.3.0 but did not update the marketplace. The installed plugin at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/punt-labs/ethos/0.2.0/ has old hooks without MCP auto-allow, dev mode detection, or new commands. The binary at ~/.local/bin/ethos is v0.3.0 with new session subcommands. Version mismatch causes session-start.sh to call session subcommands that exist in v0.3.0 binary but the hook itself is v0.2.0 code. Fix: update marketplace.json ref to v0.3.0 tag, then claude plugin update.

title: Integration design: ethos + beadle — agents with email
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and beadle. Dependency flows one way: beadle reads ethos, ethos never knows about beadle.

What it enables: agents send and receive email as themselves. Claude Agento sends from claude@punt-labs.com with GPG signing. Each agent identity has its own email address, credentials, and contact permissions.

Integration points:
- Beadle reads identity (name, handle, email field) from ethos sidecar at known filesystem paths
- Beadle stores GPG key ID, IMAP config, trust preferences in ext/beadle on the identity
- Beadle resolves identity per-request — no cached state, always reads current ethos identity
- Multi-identity: different agents in the same session can send as different addresses

Ext schema (ext/beadle):
- gpg_key_id: GPG key fingerprint for signing
- imap_host, imap_port: mail server config (if not using Proton Bridge defaults)
- trust_default: default trust level for unknown senders

Existing state: beadle already has partial ethos integration (reads email field, uses ext/beadle for GPG). Needs audit for completeness — are all integration points using the sidecar contract correctly? Does multi-identity work end-to-end?

Deliverable: design doc, ext schema definition, audit of current integration, implementation beads for gaps. No changes to ethos core.

title: PreCompact output: fix formatting, add team member talents/writing style, deduplicate
PreCompact hook emits full persona + team context
firstContentLine truncates multi-line sentences in persona opening
description: BuildPersonaBlock uses firstContentLine to extract the opening descriptor from personality markdown. When the first content line wraps across multiple lines (e.g., 'Principles from the Unix philosophy and\nMcIlroy'\''s work on software componentization.'), only the first line is extracted, producing a truncated sentence: 'Principles from the Unix philosophy and.' Fix: extract until the first sentence boundary (period followed by whitespace/newline/EOF) or until a blank line.

title: Test coverage at 2.2% — cmd/ethos, resolve, and mcp packages have 0%
Rich identity attributes: markdown references for skills, personality, writing style
description: Convert identity attributes from inline strings to markdown file references. Each attribute type gets its own directory under ~/.punt-labs/ethos/. An identity becomes a unique combination of reusable attribute files plus core identity fields (name, handle, kind, email, github, voice, agent). No backward compatibility needed — no external users. Directories: skills/, personalities/, writing-styles/. Each .md file is a self-contained definition. The identity YAML references paths. This enables reuse: two agents can share a personality but have different skills.

title: Skills as markdown files in ~/.punt-labs/ethos/skills/
description: Create skills/ directory. Each skill is a .md file defining what the identity can do, domain expertise, tools, approach. Identity YAML skills field changes from string list to path list. Examples: formal-methods.md, product-strategy.md, software-engineering.md. Same format for humans and agents — a human's skill file describes their expertise and standards, an agent's describes its capabilities and tools.

title: Personality as markdown file in ~/.punt-labs/ethos/personalities/
description: Create personalities/ directory. Each personality is a .md file defining behavioral patterns, communication tendencies, decision-making approach, values. Identity YAML personality field changes from inline string to a path. Examples: principal-engineer.md, friendly-mentor.md. Reusable — multiple identities can share a personality.

title: Writing style as markdown file in ~/.punt-labs/ethos/writing-styles/
description: Create writing-styles/ directory. Each writing style is a .md file defining written communication patterns — sentence structure, vocabulary, formatting preferences, anti-patterns, examples. Separate from personality (how you think/act) and from voice (how you sound). Identity YAML writing_style field changes from inline string to a path. Examples: concise-quantified.md, academic-formal.md.

title: Update identity schema and store for markdown attribute references
description: Update Identity struct: skills becomes []string of paths, personality becomes string path, writing_style becomes string path. Update Store to validate that referenced files exist on load. Update MCP tools (get_identity, create_identity, whoami) to resolve paths and include file contents in responses. Update ethos show to render resolved attributes. Remove inline string support — clean break.

Update both identity YAML files to reference paths instead of inline strings.

title: Installer deploys sidecar README files to ~/.punt-labs/ethos/

Use rsync or cp -r with --no-clobber to avoid overwriting user data.

title: Integration design: ethos + biff — agents with team messaging
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and biff. Dependency flows one way: biff reads ethos, ethos never knows about biff.

What it enables: agents appear in /who, send messages via /write, have real-time conversations via /talk, set plans via /plan, and are discoverable via /finger — all as their own identity.

Integration points:
- Biff reads identity (name, handle) from ethos sidecar for display name in /who and message attribution
- Biff stores preferred TTY, routing preferences, notification settings in ext/biff on the identity
- Session roster already bridges ethos and biff — biff reads participant list to know who is in the session
- /plan and /finger reflect the agent's current work and availability

Ext schema (ext/biff):
- tty: current TTY session name
- routing: message routing preferences (e.g., prefer /talk over /write)
- notifications: notification level (all, mentions, none)

Existing state: biff already reads the session roster and shows agents in /who. The ext/biff namespace exists. Needs audit: is /plan per-agent? Does /finger resolve identity correctly for agents? Does /talk work agent-to-agent?

Deliverable: design doc, ext schema definition, audit of current integration, implementation beads for gaps. No changes to ethos core.

title: refactor: consolidate CLI identity commands under identity group
description: Add ethos identity <method> subcommand group matching MCP structure. Hide old top-level shortcuts (list, show, create) except whoami. Per build-plan-projection.md Bead 2.

title: feat: wire LayeredStore into CLI and MCP
description: Replace DefaultStore() with layered factory. Update Save/Create to write repo-local by default. --global flag for user-global.

title: Plugin parity: end-to-end install + runtime fixes
feat: repo-level identity config and team resolution
description: Two capabilities bundled: (1) Move repo config from .punt-labs/ethos/config.yaml to .punt-labs/ethos.yaml — decouples config from the team submodule path. Supports agent: field (existing) and team: field (new). Works whether .punt-labs/ethos/ is a submodule or local data. (2) Team-by-repo lookup — given a repo, resolve which team works on it, who the members are, their roles, collaborations, and talents. Requires FindByRepo on team store, CLI command (ethos team for-repo), and MCP method. Together these let a repo declare its agent and team binding, and let tools query the full identity context for any repo.

title: feat: rich identity whoami/get context output
description: Identity whoami and get show a multi-line field list in panel (good) but raw JSON in context. Context should be the same formatted field list as the panel, or a richer version with resolved attribute content summaries. Raw JSON is not useful as context for the model.
